messages.text: I'll add a short plan (TODOs), then search the server code for thumbnail routes and generation.
Now searching the repo for thumbnail routes and handlers.
I'll open the server thumbnail utility to see how video files are handled.
Root cause and fix: video thumbnails are generated by FFmpeg; if FFmpeg is missing the server logs a warning and returns 404. Immediate action: install FFmpeg or set FFMPEG_PATH and restart.
Yes let's do option a
I'm going to patch the server to return a fallback SVG thumbnail and improve log details.
Patch applied: server now returns SVG placeholders and logs richer context. What's next: (1) add a test request or (2) prepare Docker change.
So if my path is: /usr/bin/ffmpeg should I set it to that in my env
Yes — set FFMPEG_PATH=/usr/bin/ffmpeg in the server env and restart the API. I showed commands for Arch and docker-compose examples.
I'm on Arch Linux
Install: sudo pacman -Syu ffmpeg; verify with which ffmpeg and ffmpeg -version; set FFMPEG_PATH if needed; restart the API.
This is my version and path
Nice — since ffmpeg is present at /usr/bin/ffmpeg, next steps: set env, restart API, verify, test thumbnail URL.
I still need to restart the API and check the new logs.
I'll mark the todo 'Reproduce error and check logs' as in-progress.
My bad, it's .arcon-thumbnails, not .arcon_thumbnails
I'll update thumbnailCacheDirectories to use the correct directory name .arcon-thumbnails only.
Done — thumbnailCacheDirectories now only includes .arcon-thumbnails. Run build and tell me if TS errors remain.

Im still getting these errors when generating missing thumbnails. The thumbnails for the videos aren't being generated. I can confirm that ffmpeg is in the env files, and it can be accessed via the application.
I'll update thumbnailMaintenance.ts to explicitly check ffmpeg availability, capture ffmpeg stderr for diagnostics, and retry thumbnailing if ffmpeg is present.
Patch applied. What I changed: re-check ffmpeg accessibility; capture stderr; simplified args. Next: restart the API and paste ffmpeg logs.
Can we add more logs for the ffmpeg?
I'll patch thumbnailMaintenance.ts to add detailed ffmpeg command, timing, stderr capture, and structured success/failure logs.
Patch applied — ffmpeg now logs start, success, failure, stderr, args, and duration.
This is the new error
I'll add logs where we mark ffmpeg unavailable and attempt to probe 'which ffmpeg' when accessibility check fails.
Patch applied.
I still need to restart the API and check the new logs.
Good — ffmpeg is present for the 'amp' user. Next: set FFMPEG_PATH and restart, or prepare static ffmpeg or docker changes. Which?
This error occurs
Mount error: create parent dir then bind or install static binary. I suggested commands to copy static ffmpeg into runner.
It seems to detect it but my amp file manager or file explorer cant see it.
Diagnosed chroot/root issues; recommend installing static ffmpeg into runner root or bind-mount host ffmpeg and libs. I provided scripts.
I think I need to do the sudo pacman --root /AMP/bun-app-runner/app -Syu ffmpeg command as the amp user
You should run package-install as root; recommended static binary method; provided scripts for both.
chroot gets permission denied
Explained permission issues and recommended static build; offered to create install script.
I want to add ffmpeg in /mnt/Servers/AMPDatastore/Instances/ARCON=API-Explorer01/bun-app-runner/app/usr/bin
I provided a safe script to download and install static ffmpeg into the runner root and verify; asked whether to generate executable script.
chroot gets permission denied
Guided to check binary type and reinstall; noted that chroot failing is okay if binary exists and will be executed by the server's process root; offered script.

I see the two files in the AMP file manager now. A different thing is that /mnt/ARCON-CLOUD/ is the folder being binded to ... The application is reading ... So /AMP/bun-app-runner/app/ is essentially /mnt/.../bun-app-runner/app/ anyways
Next: restart AMP/Bun, test generation, tail logs, verify ffmpeg execution as amp user, check thumbnail dir write perms.
it seems it slightly worked
Next checks: look for ffmpeg success/failure logs, verify thumbnail file exists, run manual ffmpeg as amp user; offered to prepare smoke-test script.
Can you export this chat to a json file
I'll write a JSON transcript file with the recent conversation to the workspace.
